headline: Dog Elected Mayor Of Small Rajasthan Town In Landslide Victory; Accepts Role Solemnly
summary: Mayor Bruno's first act was to declare all cats personae non gratae within municipal limits.
fullBody: In what political analysts are calling 'a refreshing change from the usual options,' a four-year-old golden retriever named Bruno won the mayoral election in Ramgarhia, a small town in Rajasthan's Sikar district, after receiving 847 write-in votes across two polling booths. Bruno, who was put forward as a joke candidate by a local college student group, defeated three incumbent candidates. He accepted the win with what observers described as 'great solemnity and a single slow tail wag.' His first executive action was a formal memo declaring all cats personae non gratae within municipal limits. The state government has called for a recount. The town's residents have reportedly never been more engaged in local governance.
